dict.string:
Self-Improving Agent — the closest a Shortcut gets to an auto self-improving agent, built the honest way: a scheduled memory-loop.
THE LOOP (runs itself on a weekly Automation): 1. Reads its growing MEMORY file (agent-memory.md in iCloud Drive). 2. Sends that memory + "what should I improve, and what shortcut should I build next?" to a model. 3. Shows the decision, then appends the new learning back into the memory file. Over runs, the memory grows, so each run is better-informed. The SYSTEM self-improves.
WHY THIS IS HONEST (and not a fake "autonomous agent"): • A Shortcut CANNOT rewrite its own actions — its steps are fixed. What improves is the external memory, exactly like clawhub/Hermes self-improving skills (their memory file grows; they don't rewrite themselves either). • "Auto" = a Time-of-Day Automation triggers it. There is no true unattended background autonomy on iOS, and that's by design (and safer for it). • It PROPOSES improvements and new-shortcut recipes; you act on them (hand them to Shortcut Smith to build). It can't silently install shortcuts or act without the trigger.
MORE CUSTOMIZABLE THAN SIRI / APPLE INTELLIGENCE: Siri does what Apple ships. This does what YOU wire — your memory file, your model, your logic, your data flow — and you can still trigger it by saying "Hey Siri." It extends Siri instead of being limited by it.
SETUP: create agent-memory.md in iCloud Drive; point the file actions at it. For the reasoning step, use the on-device Apple Foundation Models via the Use Model action — AFM 3 Core (Foundation Models framework), no network and no API key — or put a third-party model endpoint/key in "Get Contents of URL". Add a weekly Automation to run it.
🤖 This week's improvement + the next shortcut to build:
🤖 Memory updated. The agent got a little smarter.
